candidates.title: Unsupervised Feature Absorption Detection (UAD) with Dynamic Compensation (DFDA)
candidates.summary: First fully unsupervised absorption detection via co-occurrence clustering, paired with lightweight inference-time compensation. End-to-end detect-then-fix pipeline without supervision.
hypotheses: H1: UAD achieves F1 >= 0.6 on GPT-2 Small without probe directions
H2: UAD generalizes to Gemma-2B and Pythia-2.8B with F1 >= 0.55
H3: DFDA recovers >10% of absorbed activation with <0.01% SAE params
H4: UAD+DFDA pipeline improves downstream sparse probing accuracy
candidates.pilot_focus: Cross-model UAD validation (Gemma-2B, Pythia-2.8B) and multi-seed replication
key_risks: UAD F1 may drop below 0.5 on larger models
Co-occurrence clustering may detect correlation, not absorption specifically
DFDA improvement may not scale beyond 4 pairs
candidates.novelty_claim: First unsupervised absorption detection; first training-free compensation; first end-to-end detect-then-fix pipeline

candidates.title: Feature Co-Occurrence as General SAE Analysis Tool
candidates.summary: Generalize UAD's co-occurrence clustering beyond absorption to comprehensive unsupervised SAE analysis: hierarchy discovery, redundancy detection, community detection, anomaly detection.
hypotheses: Co-occurrence clustering reveals semantic hierarchies without supervision
Network centrality metrics identify functionally important features
Community detection on co-occurrence graphs recovers semantic categories
candidates.pilot_focus: Validate co-occurrence clustering on known semantic categories (WordNet)
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: UAD's clustering approach works on first-letter features; generalization to semantic hierarchies untested
key_risks: Semantic hierarchies may not form clean clusters in co-occurrence space
WordNet categories may not align with neural network feature organization
candidates.novelty_claim: General-purpose co-occurrence analysis toolkit for SAEs; extends 'Geometry of Concepts' to practical tool
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD F1 < 0.45 on larger models but co-occurrence structure is still informative

candidates.title: Absorption as a Consequence of SAE Nonidentifiability
candidates.summary: Challenge the field's framing by showing absorption patterns are unstable across random seeds, suggesting absorption is an artifact of nonidentifiability rather than a real feature property.
hypotheses: Absorption patterns vary significantly across random seeds for the same concept
Feature overlap across seeds correlates negatively with absorption stability
The absorption-hedging classification is not reproducible across initializations
candidates.pilot_focus: Train 3-5 SAEs with different seeds, measure absorption stability for first-letter features
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: EleutherAI found ~30% feature overlap across seeds; absorption stability untested
key_risks: May be dismissed as 'gotcha' paper without constructive recommendations
If absorption IS stable across seeds, hypothesis is falsified
candidates.novelty_claim: First systematic study of absorption stability across seeds; challenges field's framing
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD detects different absorption patterns across seeds; high seed variance observed

candidates.title: Absorption as SAE Quality Diagnostic
candidates.summary: Treat absorption rate as a leading indicator of overall SAE quality. High absorption correlates with poor seed consistency, low auto-interpretability, and high dead feature ratios.
hypotheses: Absorption rate negatively correlates with seed consistency (Hungarian matching overlap)
Absorption rate negatively correlates with auto-interpretability scores
Absorption rate positively correlates with dead feature ratio
candidates.pilot_focus: Train SAEs with varying hyperparameters, measure absorption vs quality metrics
candidates.evidence_from_iter1: Dead feature ratios were extremely high (94-99%) in trained SAEs; relationship to absorption untested
key_risks: Correlations may be weak or confounded by hyperparameter choices
Quality metrics themselves may be unreliable
candidates.novelty_claim: Reframes absorption from 'problem to fix' to 'signal to monitor'
candidates.pivot_trigger: UAD fails but absorption rate still varies meaningfully across SAE configurations

pivot_criteria.if_uad_f1_below_0.5: Pivot to cand_b, cand_c, or cand_d based on observed failure mode
pivot_criteria.if_uad_f1_0.5_to_0.55: Proceed with cand_a but add cand_b as secondary contribution

synthesis_notes: Iteration 1 evidence decisively inverts the original contribution hierarchy. The exploratory hypotheses (UAD/DFDA) produced robust, novel results while the primary hypotheses (CAAB, causal assessment) were inconclusive due to proxy metrics and underpowered designs. The front-runner is the only candidate with both novelty and empirical support. Backups provide escape routes if cross-model validation fails.
